_meta.usage: Maps to the ScriptableObjects in the design doc (ChapterDefinition, GeneratorDefinition, UpgradeDefinition, EventDefinition, BarDefinition, RewardDefinition, AlbumPrestige config). Either hand-author SOs from these values or write a small importer. ALL ScriptableObjects are discovered at runtime through Addressables (labels per type), not direct references. All numbers are starter values for tuning; the Records capstone gate is the primary pacing knob.

modelNotes.conditions: Every gate/unlock/visibility/availability rule is a single discriminated Condition shape — always an object with a `type` field — evaluated by ONE ConditionEvaluator. No bare/heterogeneous shapes. Types used in Ch1: `currency` (current balance ≥ amount), `currencyEarnedTotal` (lifetime earned ≥ value), `ownedCount` (generator owned ≥ value), `flagSet` (a flag is set), `barsCompleted` (≥ value bars completed in a group), `recordsCumulative` (cumulative Records ≥ value), `compound` (all/any of nested Conditions).
modelNotes.flags: One flag registry drives every progressive reveal. A content-unlock upgrade's payload is `setFlag`; a reward's payload can also be `setFlag`; sections, currencies, bar groups, and any gated content reveal on a `flagSet` Condition. There is no separate `unlockSystem` code path — revealing a system = setting its flag.
modelNotes.bars: A 'Learn Covers' cover is a generic fillable BAR. A bar declares `fillCurrency` (a currency id) + `fillRequirement` + `reward`; the fill logic reads `fillCurrency` and never hardcodes covers. Rehearsal is an ordinary run-scoped currency with its own earn config (per-sec tick + per-tap yield). Bars and their fill currency meet only through the `fillCurrency` id — mirrors how generators declare `produces`. A group also declares `delivery` (how currency moves from pool to bar: `continuous` in Ch1) as per-group data, so a chapter that wants tap-to-pour is a data change, not a new system.
modelNotes.addressables: Chapters, Generators, Upgrades, Events, Bars, Currencies, CurrencyGroups, and Rewards are all loaded by Addressables label. Managers build their registries from whatever assets carry the label — no hardcoded lists, no direct scene/asset references.

currencies.cash: run-scoped; earned by tap + generators; spent on gear/upgrades
currencies.fans: run-scoped; performance meter; sets the album (demo) Records payout; loosely coupled to Cash by design
currencies.rehearsal: run-scoped; earned by a passive tick + taps; fills bars (Learn Covers) via each bar's fillCurrency
currencies.records: PERMANENT; each Record buffs global income; cumulative Records gate the capstone

chapter.name: Garage / Basement
chapter.theme: The band forms in a garage and plays its first backyard show.
chapter.storyBeatOpen: It starts in the garage. Just you, a beat-up amp, and a handful of songs you half-know. Time to make some noise.
chapter.storyBeatCapstone: The backyard's packed. You plug in, count off, and for three minutes the whole neighborhood is yours. Someone's already asking when the next one is — and a guy named Dave offers to haul your gear. You've got your first roadie.

constants.incomeFormulaNote: globalIncome = sum(gen.baseOutput * gen.count * genBuffs) * (1 + 0.02 * records) * encoreBoost. Catalog/Roadie multipliers are not present in Ch1.

flags.reveals: Rehearsal currency + Learn Covers bar group

flagsNote: The single reveal registry. Flags are permanent-in-chapter. A content-unlock upgrade (or a reward) sets a flag via a `setFlag` payload; anything that should appear when a system exists gates on `{ "type": "flagSet", "flag": <id> }`.

sections.name: The Garage Floor
sections.note: No visibleWhen: visible from chapter start. Module ids are Addressable keys resolved through a string→prefab registry loaded by label; a section reveals as a group when its visibleWhen Condition holds (progressive reveal, design doc §2).

sections.name: The Band
sections.note: Appears with the Stage-0 goal; the practice_amp row unlocks at the same moment. Later sections gate on flags, e.g. { "type": "flagSet", "flag": "fans" }.

sections.name: The Rehearsal Space
sections.note: The bars region: Learn Covers rows plus the Rehearsal readout. Gates on the covers flag (set by learn_covers at 25 Fans) — the same flag that activates Rehearsal accrual and reveals the bar group, so the whole system appears as one moment.

generators.name: Practice Amp
generators.note: First automation. Unlocks the instant the Stage-0 goal (earn 100 Cash by tapping) is met.

generators.name: Drummer
generators.note: First bandmate. Buying the first one satisfies the play_for_crowd gate, which sets the `fans` flag.

upgrades.name: Tight Set
upgrades.note: Gated on Fans, not Cash — demonstrates the gating web (§4). Same Condition shape as a Cash gate, just a different `currency` id, so no special-casing.

upgrades.name: Play for a Crowd
upgrades.note: Stage 2. Recruiting your first Drummer sets the `fans` flag — which reveals the Fans currency and meter.

upgrades.name: Learn Some Covers
upgrades.note: Stage 3. Sets the `covers` flag — reveals the Rehearsal currency and the Learn Covers bar group.

upgrades.name: Cut a Demo
upgrades.note: Stage 4. Sets the `album` flag — reveals the Release/Cut-a-Demo (prestige) button. This is the core teaching moment.

rewardsNote: The shared reward pool. Bars and event tiers reference these by id; each type maps to a RewardDefinition subclass asset, so a new reward kind is a new type + entries, and rewards are reusable across content. Available types: fanRateMultiplier, tapValueMultiplier, setFlag (payload `flag: <id>` — lets a reward reveal gated content through the same flag registry as content-unlock upgrades).

rehearsal.note: Rehearsal is a plain run-scoped currency, revealed by the `covers` flag. It accrues from a passive tick (perSec) plus Jam taps (perTap) — so it advances from engagement, not from Cash. Modelled like `fans`: the currency owns its earn config; consumers (bars) reference it by id.

groups.name: Learn Covers
groups.deliveryNote: How fill currency moves from the pool into the target — orthogonal to fillMode's bookkeeping. `continuous`: accrued Rehearsal streams into the group's active (player-selected) bar as it arrives; the pool only holds a balance when no bar is selected. Future modes (tap a fixed chunk, dump the pool) are new delivery values, not new systems.
groups.fillModeNote: perBar / player-directed: each bar accrues its OWN progress by draining the shared Rehearsal pool; the player chooses which bar to pour Rehearsal into. Bars are independent (not cumulative thresholds on one counter). Totals: 120 + 300 + 600 = 1020 Rehearsal to finish all three.

bars.note: A bar is a generic fillable: it declares `fillCurrency` + `fillRequirement` + `reward`. The fill system reads `fillCurrency` and works for any currency — covers are just the Ch1 instance. Run-scoped: bars reset each demo but refill faster as the run gets stronger. cover_1 is on the critical path (its completion satisfies the barsCompleted leg of cut_demo). Bar rewards stack multiplicatively on fan rate.

fans.perBandmateNote: Applies per owned unit of any generator with `isBandmate: true` (behavior-as-data — the fan system reads the flag, it does not name drummer/bassist/guitarist). Ties fan rate to band size.
fans.couplingNote: fanRate is intentionally NOT a function of Cash or Cash/sec — only band size, bar rewards, and time — so income alone cannot shortcut the demo payout (§11).

album.note: Releasing resets the run and awards Records. Reset is driven by currency group `resetsOnAlbumRelease` + upgrade/bar `scope`, not a hardcoded name list. Flags are kept (content stays revealed across demos). Each Record = +2% permanent global income, so the next run is faster.

events.name: Garage Jam Challenge
events.availableNote: Introduced in Stage 6, after the player has released at least one demo, so the prestige loop is understood first. Same `recordsCumulative` Condition type the capstone uses — one evaluator, different threshold.
events.baselineResetNote: On entry the chapter economy resets to a fixed baseline for the event's duration, so tiers scale independently of the player's Records.
events.failNote: On a timed fail the event resets; the player can quit anytime. Cost is time only, never permanent progress.
events.tuningNote: Reward magnitude is the dial that sets how essential this event is (§6.1). As written it's a helpful-but-skippable tap booster; raise the reward to make it soft-required for reasonable pacing.

onComplete.roadieNote: First Roadie is a story reward here. The allocation/replay system stays locked until Chapter 2.

balanceTargets.primaryPacingKnob: capstoneRecordsGate — raise/lower before touching generator or fan curves
balanceTargets.tuneAgainst: a player with full multiplier stack active (here just Encore up to 4x + records); confirm the chapter still takes meaningful play time
